organism: Caulobacter vibrioides (strain ATCC 19089 / CIP 103742 / CB 15) (Caulobacter crescentus)

machinery_genes.role: inner-membrane ABC transporter / ATPase that energises export and forms the IM channel
machinery_genes.quote: The first ORF was 1,656 bp long and started 242 bp after the termination codon of rsaA... The gene was designated rsaD because of its similarity to these genes
machinery_genes.note: Awram & Smit 1998 identified two genes immediately 3' of rsaA by Tn5 mutagenesis. RsaD is the ABC transporter, named for similarity to AprD (Pseudomonas aeruginosa alkaline protease) and PrtB/PrtD (Erwinia chrysanthemi metalloprotease) type I transporters. Located in the rsaADE cluster.

machinery_genes.role: periplasm-spanning IM-anchored adaptor bridging the ABC transporter to the outer-membrane channel
machinery_genes.quote: The second ORF started 146 bp after rsaD... The gene was designated rsaE because of its similarity to these genes
machinery_genes.note: Awram & Smit 1998. RsaE is the membrane fusion protein (MFP/HlyD-family adaptor), named for similarity to AprE and PrtE of the analogous P. aeruginosa and E. chrysanthemi type I systems. Second gene of the rsaADE cluster, downstream of rsaD.

machinery_genes.role: TolC-family outer-membrane channel forming the exit duct (primary OMP)
machinery_genes.quote: The genes rsaF(a) and rsaF(b) were identified by similarity to the Escherichia coli hemolysin secretion OMP TolC by using the C. crescentus genome sequence.
machinery_genes.note: Toporowski et al 2004. RsaFa is the TolC-family OMP; rsaF(a) lies downstream of the rsaADE genes after a ~5 kb gap. RsaFa has 23% identity / 45% similarity to E. coli TolC. rsaF(a) knockout retained ~56% of wild-type secretion.

machinery_genes.role: second TolC-family outer-membrane channel required for full-level secretion
machinery_genes.quote: The genes rsaF(a) and rsaF(b) were identified by similarity to the Escherichia coli hemolysin secretion OMP TolC by using the C. crescentus genome sequence.
machinery_genes.note: Toporowski et al 2004. RsaFb is a second, unlinked TolC-family OMP (25% identity / 47% similarity to E. coli TolC), located ~322 kb from rsaFa. Both rsaF genes are required for full secretion; double knockout abolishes RsaA secretion. This is the only known T1SS requiring two OMPs to assemble a fully functional system.

notes: System: the Caulobacter crescentus CB15 S-layer secretion machinery, a type I (ABC) secretion system that exports RsaA, the paracrystalline S-layer subunit, to the cell surface via an uncleaved C-terminal signal. This is the same C. crescentus T1SS that matches the effector context (CC_1007 = rsaA, the S-layer protein, which is the secreted substrate and is NOT listed here). All three canonical T1SS components are documented gene-by-gene in the literature on this exact organism (no strain transfer needed): the ABC transporter RsaD and membrane fusion protein RsaE were identified immediately 3' of rsaA by Awram & Smit 1998 (J Bacteriol, DOI 10.1128/jb.180.12.3062-3069.1998), forming the rsaADE cluster; the TolC-family OMP component is unusual in being split across two genes, rsaFa (downstream of rsaADE) and rsaFb (unlinked, ~322 kb away), both characterized by Toporowski et al 2004 (J Bacteriol, DOI 10.1128/JB.186.23.8000-8009.2004). RsaFa and RsaFb are both counted as machinery because both are required for full-level secretion and both are assigned to this T1SS by the paper. Deliberately excluded: RsaA itself (the secreted S-layer substrate, = effector CC_1007), and the other unlinked rsa/sap S-layer-glycosylation/maturation genes (not transport apparatus). No escort chaperone or dedicated transcriptional regulator is listed. DOIs and PMIDs verified via get_article_metadata (9620954, 15547272, 20657621 all resolve). The 1998 and 2004 PMC full texts had gene names stripped by the JATS parser; verbatim naming quotes were recovered from the PMC HTML (rsaD/rsaE designation sentences from PMC107805; rsaF(a)/rsaF(b)/TolC sentence from PMC529074).
